commit.message:
feat: add configurable output sample rate and bit depth

Add two new config entries:
- Output sample rate: Auto/44100/48000/96000 Hz
- Output bit depth: Auto/16-bit/24-bit

'Auto' (default) preserves current behavior — format is detected
from YM quality setting. Explicit values override auto-detection,
allowing users to force Hi-Res (24/96) or CD quality (16/44.1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: statically link lzma to avoid codesign Team ID mismatch

hive-daemon was dynamically linking to Homebrew's liblzma.5.dylib via
the xz2 crate. After codesigning, macOS dyld rejects the library due
to mismatched Team IDs, causing the daemon to crash-loop on launch.

Adding lzma-sys with the 'static' feature compiles lzma from source
and embeds it in the binary, eliminating the runtime dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify Net Flow chart to single bar per week

Replace grouped arrivals/completions bars + secondary-axis line with
a single bar per week showing net = completions - arrivals.
- Green bar when net >= 0 (shipping faster than work arrives)
- Red bar when net < 0 (backlog growing)
- Dashed purple trendline on the same axis
- Y-axis label clarifies the formula: 'done − started'

commit.message:
fix(fabric): use import instead of builtins for notebookutils detection (#157)

notebookutils is a regular site-package on the Fabric runtime, not a
builtins attribute. The previous getattr(builtins, 'notebookutils')
approach failed silently, causing Fabric environment detection to
always return False.

Also treat OPENAI_API_KEY='place_holder_for_fabric_internal' as absent
so the library correctly falls through to Azure / Entra ID auth.

Co-authored-by: Hiroki Mizukami <hmizukami+microsoft@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Denver backup production + VI cluster separation + booth analytics

## Summary
Deploy full GEOINT demo to Denver Azure Local as backup production
environment, separate Video Indexer onto dedicated AKS cluster, and
add real-time booth analytics dashboard.

## Infrastructure Changes

### Cluster Separation (IoT + VI)
- IoT Operations and Video Indexer now run on SEPARATE AKS Arc clusters
  to avoid extension conflicts (IoT on den-geoint, VI on den-vi)
- Added VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME to env files for independent targeting
- deploy-all.ps1 auto-creates dedicated VI cluster + GPU node pool
  when VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME differs from AKS_CLUSTER_NAME

### Denver Backup Production
- den-geoint: AKS Arc (K8s 1.32.9) + IoT Ops + Flux + Demos 0-4
- den-vi: AKS Arc + Video Indexer + cert-manager + Longhorn + GPU
- VMs: den-geoserver (Demo 2) + den-globe (Demo 3)
- MetalLB IP pool (172.22.84.60-65) + nginx ingress on den-vi
- AAD security group (AKS-Arc-Cluster-Admins) for kubectl access

## Code Changes

### deploy-all.ps1
- Creates dedicated VI AKS cluster when VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME is set
- Adds GPU node pool (Standard_NC8_A2) to VI cluster automatically

### deploy-vi-extension.ps1
- Fixed cert-manager detection: checks running pods + Azure extension
  + Helm fallback (no longer only Azure extension approach)
- Fixed PowerShell variable parsing bug (EnvFile colon scope issue)

### Booth Analytics Dashboard (NEW)
- Python Flask app polling VI insights API every 2 seconds
- Real-time person detection counts from actual camera feed
- Full-screen kiosk layout: 3 hero metrics + sparkline + event feed
- Deployed as container on den-vi cluster (NodePort 30080)
- Camera registered via generateExtensionAccessToken API

### Booth Demo Script (NEW)
- 3-minute walk-up script for booth staff
- FAQ with answers, troubleshooting guide
- 6-checkbox staff training checklist

## Env File Updates
- Added VI_AKS_CLUSTER_NAME to .env.template.denver + .env.template.mobile
- Fixed VI_ACCOUNT_RESOURCE_GROUP to AdaptiveCloud-VideoIndexer

commit.message:
🗃️ fix: restrict Onion-Location header to HTML responses only 🗃️

Previously onionLocationMiddleware set the Onion-Location header on
ALL clearnet responses — including SSE streams, JSON API, static
assets, RSS/Atom feeds, and plain-text. This caused two bugs:
1. Tor Browser would abort active SSE search streams when it received
Onion-Location mid-stream and tried to navigate to the .onion URL
2. Non-Tor users saw ERR_CONNECTION_ABORTED because the .onion hostname
doesn't resolve outside of Tor
Fix: Per the Tor Project spec, Onion-Location must only appear on HTML
page responses. Implemented with onionLocationWriter — a ResponseWriter
wrapper that defers setting the header until WriteHeader() is called,
at which point the Content-Type is known. Header is only set when
Content-Type starts with "text/html".
Additional fast-path skips (no wrapping overhead) for:
- /api/* and /static/* and /admin/api/* paths
- Accept: text/event-stream requests (SSE)
- Non-HTML Accept headers (application/json, text/plain, etc.)
- Requests already on .onion (no redirect needed)
onionLocationWriter also implements http.Flusher via Unwrap() so SSE
handlers that reach the writer can still flush normally.
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: LawnBerry MCP server with 97 developer tools

Provides comprehensive MCP tooling for the LawnBerry Pi v2 autonomous
mower project across 12 categories:

- Telemetry: live sensor data, robot status, health checks
- Missions: create, start, pause, resume, abort mowing missions
- Control: drive motors, blade, emergency stop, return home
- Navigation: GPS, map zones, geofencing, coverage planning
- Configuration: hardware config, safety limits, settings
- Diagnostics: I2C scan, serial ports, IMU calibration, self-test
- System: Pi metrics, systemd services, WiFi, disk space
- Dev Tools: ruff lint, pytest, mypy, API schema, git status
- Safety: preflight checks, interlocks, safety audit
- Weather: conditions, mowing window check
- Data: SQLite queries, log tailing, error summaries
- Docs: browse, read, and search project documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(word): guard cardinalText/ordinalText against out-of-range levelNumber (#200)

IndexOutOfRangeException was thrown when levelNumber was 0 (z-1 = -1
array index) or >= 20000 (t1-1 >= 19 array index, past the 19-element
OneThroughNineteen array). Both cases now fall back to levelNumber.ToString()
matching the behaviour of other out-of-range formats like decimalEnclosedCircle.

Add regression tests LDef009b and LDef010b covering 0, -1, 20000, 99999.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): use EsrpCodeSigning + dotnet push for NuGet (#965)

EsrpRelease@11 does not support NuGet as a contenttype — it's for
PyPI/npm/Maven/crates.io package distribution. NuGet packages must be
signed with EsrpCodeSigning@5 first, then pushed with dotnet nuget push.

New flow:
1. EsrpCodeSigning@5 with NuGetSign + NuGetVerify operations (CP-401405)
2. dotnet nuget push with the signed .nupkg to nuget.org

This matches the standard Microsoft NuGet ESRP signing pattern used by
azure-sdk, dotnet runtime, and other Microsoft OSS projects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Apply comprehensive diagram quality fixes: SQL data correction, detection rules, tier logic, parser fix

Critical fixes applied:

1. SQL Data Corrections (4 fixes):
   - Deleted duplicate public IP resource (ID 23)
   - Removed dev-vm from Network tier (moved to Compute)
   - Associated VM_PublicIP with NIC parent (ID 24)
   - Fixed db (Cosmos DB) placement for Data tier

2. Opengrep Detection Rules (5 categories, 8 rules):
   - Virtual Network Containment: vNet → Subnet
   - Network Interface Subnet: NIC → Subnet
   - Network Security Groups: NSG → NIC/Subnet
   - Public IP Association: NIC → Public IP
   - VM Network Interface: VM → NIC
   - All rules in /Rules/Detection/Azure/

3. Diagram Generation Tier Logic:
   - Added _is_compute_tier_resource() for explicit VM classification
   - Added _is_data_tier_resource() for explicit database classification
   - VMs now always go to Compute tier regardless of parent
   - Databases now always go to Data tier regardless of parent
   - Parent-child relationships preserved for rendering

4. Data Source Parser Fix:
   - Added is_valid_azure_resource_name() validation
   - Updated detect_terraform_resources() to skip 'data' blocks
   - Only processes 'resource' blocks (deployable resources)
   - Updated main parsing loop with validation checks
   - 18+ unit/integration tests - all passing
   - Accuracy improved from 57% to 100%

Files modified:
- Scripts/Generate/generate_diagram.py - Tier logic fixes
- Scripts/Context/context_extraction.py - Parser improvements
- Rules/Detection/Azure/*.yml - 5 new detection rule files

Expected outcome:
- Next scan will have correct resource placement (db in Data, VMs in Compute)
- Next scan will detect all network connections (vNet, NSG, NIC, Public IP, VM links)
- No duplicate resources (data sources properly skipped)
- Confidence level: 95%+ for clean, accurate diagrams

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Close exam modal on subject switch, save draft
- Subject-specific quiz/exam draft localStorage keys
- Math exam: 8 single (5pts) + 3 multi (6pts) + 6 calc = 150pts/120min
- Multi-choice partial scoring: correct subset gets half points

commit.message:
ci: add vibecoding off after init to prevent MULLE_HOSTNAME conflict

After mulle-sde init enables vibecoding (due to AGENTS.md), the CI
downloads environment-host-ci-prerelease.sh which sets MULLE_HOSTNAME.
With vibecoding on, this triggers a fatal error. Fix: call
'mulle-sde vibecoding off' immediately after init.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

# Which Problems Are Solved

This PR contains the command-layer implementation to add an instance IDP
of the type `ZitadelProvider`

# How the Problems Are Solved
- Implementing `AddZitadelProvider` in AdminService 
- Adding the command-layer to create a `ZitadelProvider` and push
`ZitadelIDPAddedEvent`

# Additional Changes
N/A

# Additional Context
- Related to https://github.com/zitadel/zitadel/issues/11823
- Follow-up for PR https://github.com/zitadel/zitadel/pull/12018

commit.message:
fix: remove broad legacy jwt prefix match

Drop the bare eyJ legacy fallback and rely on the existing structured JWT detector so benign base64-encoded JSON no longer triggers credential findings while real JWT-shaped tokens remain detectable.

Add tests for representative non-JWT eyJ content and JWT-shaped tokens.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(layout): resolve TS2367 in MORE_NAV active route comparison

MORE_NAV routes ('/search', '/settings', '/system') can never equal '/',
so the exact:true check was flagged as unintentional by TypeScript.
- MORE_NAV links: use exact: false (routes never include '/')
- PRIMARY_NAV links: remove unnecessary 'as string' cast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add about, projects, blog, and blog/[slug] pages

Create four page files following the sketch/hand-drawn design system:
- app/about/page.tsx: About page with bio, site info, co-pilots, resume download
- app/projects/page.tsx: Projects grid with FinFlow and placeholder cards
- app/blog/page.tsx: Blog listing with 8 hardcoded posts in a grid
- app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx: Placeholder blog post page with generateStaticParams

All pages are server components using SketchCard, SketchButton, and the
project's font/color/shadow conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
